content.full_text:
February 6, 1978

78-2d-104

Ms. Robin Young
Friends of Robin Young
591 Magnolia Way
La Habra, California  90631

Dear Robin:

Since you will not be sending your Womens Rights Handbook through the mail the identification requirement outlined in section 84305 of the Government Code is not applicable. You may, however, be required to comply with the provisions of the Elections Code Section 29410 which covers all material having reference to an election other than mass mailings covered by the Political Reform Act. So I would suggest you either contact your local district attorney or the Attorney General's Office (Scott Thorpe, 916/445-6808) for information concerning identification requirements under the Elections Code.

In response to your second question, an assembly candidate is a candidate for state office and therefore, all mass mailings sent by the candidates or committees supporting or opposing a state candidate must be sent to the Commission.

It was very good to hear from you. I hope you will let me know if I can be of assistance in the future.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

30

Friends of Robin Young
591 Magnolia Way
La Habra, CA. 90631
January 16, 1978


Ms. Regina Siciliano Kutohina
F.P.P.C.
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Regina;

I am starting to campaign for the 69th Assembly district.
As part of my campaign, I plan to be distributing to womens
groups the Handbook on Womens Rights put out by Evelle
Younger's office. Naturally I would like to put a stamp or
sticker on the inside which which say something like
"Complements of Robin Young for Assembly". As we did not
pay anything for the booklet, nor did we print it, I need
to know what we must put on the booklet in the way of committe name, address, etc. We will not send it through the mail.
I would appreciate a written opinion on this.

Also, the new manual dated December 9, 1977 says in paragraph
six, page 11 that a state candidate must send a copy of
every major mailing to the Commission. Is an Assembly
candidate a state candidate?

I would greatly appreciate some advice on these two items.
Hope all has been well with you.

Sincerely,

Robin

Robin Young

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked two questions: (1) whether identification requirements under Government Code Section 84305 apply to a Women's Rights Handbook sent by mail, and (2) whether an assembly candidate is considered a candidate for state office requiring mass mailing disclosures to the Commission.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that Section 84305 identification requirements do not apply to the Women's Rights Handbook, but Elections Code Section 410 may apply; the requestor should contact the district attorney or Attorney General for Elections Code questions. An assembly candidate is a state office candidate, so all mass mailings by such candidates or supporting/opposing committees must be sent to the Commission.

Content reconstructed from fragmented text. Two distinct advisory responses provided regarding different aspects of political reform compliance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked two questions: (1) whether identification requirements under Government Code Section 84305 apply to a Women's Rights Handbook sent by mail, and (2) whether an assembly candidate is considered a candidate for state office requiring mass mailing disclosures to the Commission.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that Section 84305 identification requirements do not apply to the Women's Rights Handbook, but Elections Code Section 410 may apply; the requestor should contact the district attorney or Attorney General for Elections Code questions. An assembly candidate is a state office candidate, so all mass mailings by such candidates or supporting/opposing committees must be sent to the Commission.
